title: Will Bitcoin reach $100,000 in 2026?
rules: Resolves YES if Bitcoin price exceeds $100,000 USD on CoinGecko at any point before December 31, 2026 11:59 PM ET.

title: Will inflation fall below 2% in 2026?
rules: Resolves YES if the Bureau of Labor Statistics reports a 12-month CPI-U change below 2.0% for any month with a reference period in 2026. Based on seasonally adjusted figures.
notes: Seasonally adjusted vs non-adjusted is a hidden technicality. BLS-specific source.

title: Will the Fed cut rates in March 2026?
rules: Resolves YES if the Federal Open Market Committee announces a decrease in the target federal funds rate at its March 2026 meeting. Resolution is based on the FOMC statement released at the conclusion of the meeting.
notes: Clean — specific meeting, specific action, clear source.

title: Will a Marvel movie gross $1B in 2026?
rules: Resolves YES if any Marvel Cinematic Universe film released in 2026 reaches $1,000,000,000 in worldwide box office gross according to Box Office Mojo by January 15, 2027.
notes: End date extends to Jan 15 2027 — retail assumes 2026 year-end. Box Office Mojo specific.

title: Will unemployment rise above 5%?
rules: Resolves YES if the U.S. unemployment rate (U-3, seasonally adjusted) as reported by the Bureau of Labor Statistics exceeds 5.0% for any month in 2026. Initial release figures are used; revisions are not considered.
notes: Initial release vs revised is a hidden technicality. BLS specific.

title: Will Trump win the 2028 Republican primary?
rules: Resolves YES if Donald Trump is the Republican Party nominee for President in 2028 as determined by the Associated Press.
notes: AP-specific resolution. AP may take time to call. 'Win primary' vs 'be nominee' is subtle.

title: Will S&P 500 close above 6000 in Q1 2026?
rules: Resolves YES if the S&P 500 Index closing price exceeds 6,000.00 on any trading day between January 1 and March 31, 2026, as reported by S&P Global.
notes: Clean — closing price clearly specified, date range matches title.

title: Will California ban gas cars by 2030?
rules: Resolves YES if the California Air Resources Board implements a regulation that prohibits the sale of new gasoline-powered passenger vehicles effective on or before December 31, 2030. The regulation must be formally adopted, not merely proposed.
notes: Ban vs regulate. 'New' only — not all gas cars. 'Formally adopted' vs 'proposed' distinction.

title: Will gas prices hit $5/gallon nationally?
rules: Resolves YES if the U.S. Energy Information Administration's weekly retail gasoline price (regular grade, national average) reaches or exceeds $5.000 per gallon at any point in 2026.
notes: EIA weekly average vs daily spot. Regular grade only. National average masks regional spikes.

title: Will Elon Musk become a trillionaire in 2026?
rules: Resolves YES if Elon Musk's net worth exceeds $1,000,000,000,000 USD according to the Bloomberg Billionaires Index at any point before December 31, 2026 11:59 PM ET.
notes: Bloomberg Billionaires Index specific — Forbes or other sources won't count.

title: Will GPT-5 be released in 2026?
rules: Resolves YES if OpenAI publicly releases a model officially designated as 'GPT-5' (or successor) to the general public before December 31, 2026. Limited previews, API-only access, or beta programs do not count.
notes: 'General public' release vs API access. 'Or successor' is ambiguous. Beta exclusion matters.

title: Will there be a government shutdown in 2026?
rules: Resolves YES if a lapse in appropriations causes a partial or full shutdown of the U.S. federal government lasting at least 24 hours during calendar year 2026.
notes: 24-hour minimum — brief technical lapses that get resolved overnight won't count.
